etymology.description: 山(山) (shān, "hill") + 石(石) (shí, "rock") -> depicting a craggy hill or cliff -> rock; cliff
etymology.interpretation: The character 岩 combines 山(山) (shān, "hill") representing the elevation and 石(石) (shí, "rock") indicating the stony nature to concretely depict a rocky hill or cliff. This visual metaphor naturally leads to the meaning of 'rock' and 'cliff'.

examples.chinese: 山顶有一个巨大岩石(山頂有一個巨大巖石)。
examples.pinyin: Shāndǐng yǒu yí gè jùdà yánshí.
examples.english: There is a huge rock at the mountain top.

examples.chinese: 他在岩壁上攀爬(他在巖壁上攀爬)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài yánbì shàng pān pá.
examples.english: He is climbing on the rock wall.

examples.chinese: 这条路沿着岩石悬崖(這條路沿着巖石懸崖)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù yánzhe yánshí xuányá.
examples.english: This road runs along the rocky cliff.
